title: The Little Cripple's Complaint by Ann Taylor — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
I'm a helpless cripple child,
Gentle Christians, pity me;
Once, in rosy health I smiled,
Blithe and gay as you can be,
And upon the village green
First in every sport was seen.

Now, alas! I'm weak and low,
meaning: "The Little Cripple's Complaint" by Ann Taylor is a deep poem consisting of 48 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "I'm a helpless cripple child, Gentle Christians, pity me;...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ann Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
